commentaries.author: Eusebius of Caesarea
commentaries.content: Notice how in this passage he does not call those of the circumcision to hear the un-speakable words, but only those whom he calls “forsaken and tortured.” These were those of the time of the apostles who regretted and lamented the evil of humanity. - "Proof of the Gospel 2.3"

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Floor: you who must shortly be reduced to the utmost distress. Baladan was friendly to Ezechias. But Assar don having seized Babylon, took Manasses prisoner; and the city thenceforward continued to fill up the measure of its sins. (Calmet)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
